plan_adherence_notes: All 9 plan steps executed faithfully. 14 new test methods cover all planned scenarios: 5 POST validation errors, 1 market-not-active rejection, 1 idempotency replay, 1 idempotency conflict, 1 GET basic with sort/pagination, 3 GET pagination variants, 2 GET edge cases. Helper extensions match plan exactly. Minor note: GET basic test only asserts 3 comments (matching posts in that test), not verifying isolation from other tests' comments — but this is fine since BayesMarketApiAuthRateLimitTests uses setUp/tearDown to reset state between tests.
missed_questions: Should tests verify comment content fields (e.g., accountId, body, createdAt, seq) in the POST 201 response beyond just status code?
Should there be a test for rate limiting on the comment POST endpoint specifically?
reason: All planned tests implemented and passing (15 comment tests, 283 total). Research was thorough with correct assumptions. Plan was well-structured with clear dependencies. No regressions introduced. The two missed questions are minor — response field verification is partially covered by the idempotency replay test (which asserts payload2['comment'] == payload1['comment']), and rate limiting is covered by the existing rate-limit test infrastructure in the same class.
